name: hot_all_day_no_nat_vent_window
description: Outdoor is already above indoor from the start of the day. Warm-day classification fires at 6 AM with outdoor=76F > indoor=72F. A sensor_open event immediately enters pause (not nat vent) because outdoor >= indoor would add heat. No natural_ventilation outcome fires at any point.
notes: Covers the 'no nat-vent window' pattern: outdoor above indoor all day.
On days where outdoor is already warmer than indoor, opening windows adds heat rather than cooling.
The nat-vent activation guard (outdoor < indoor) prevents nat vent even when windows are opened.
HVAC off is applied by classification; the user has no beneficial window-open time.
Pairs with hot_all_day cases where ceiling guard would fire (modeled in Group B).

events.note: Already warm: outdoor=76F above indoor=72F before sunrise.
Warm-day classification: hvac_mode=off, windows not recommended (outdoor already above indoor).
Mid-morning: outdoor 79F, indoor 73F. Outdoor continues climbing above indoor.
User opens a window despite warm outdoor — outdoor 79F > indoor 73F should trigger pause, not nat vent.

assertions.reason: Warm-day classification applied: hvac_mode=off. Indoor=72F is above comfort_heat=70F so no comfort-floor guard fires.
sensor_open with outdoor=79F >= indoor=73F — opening windows would add heat, so automation pauses instead of activating nat vent.
All sensors closed — automation resumes from pause.

verdict.summary: When outdoor is above indoor all day, window-open events enter pause not nat vent; no natural_ventilation fires
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (off) → paused on window open → resumed on window close
verdict.expected_behavior: Pause when outdoor >= indoor; nat vent guard correctly suppressed all day
